name: Arr
comment:
An `Arr` describes an array where you know more about the amount of elements.

    Array.empty |> Array.get 0
    --> Nothing

    Arr.empty |> Arr.at nat0 FirstToLast
    --> compile time error

Is this any useful? Let's look at an example:

> 0 to 100 joined by a space

    joinBy between =
        \before after -> before ++ between ++ after

    let
        intStringsAfter =
            Array.fromList (List.range 1 100)
                String.fromInt
    in
    Array.foldl (joinBy " ") "0" intStringsAfter
    --> "0 1 2 3 4 5 ..."

    Arr.nats nat100
        |> Arr.map (val >> String.fromInt)
        |> Arr.foldWith FirstToLast (joinBy " ")
    --> "0 1 2 3 4 5 ..."

The `Array` version just seems hacky and is less readable. `Arr` simply knows more about the length at compile time, so you can e.g. call `foldWith` without a worry.

@docs Arr


## create

@docs fromArray, repeat, nats, random


### exact

@docs empty, from1

[Skip to `from16`](Arr#from16).

@docs from2, from3, from4, from5, from6, from7, from8, from9, from10, from11, from12, from13, from14, from15, from16

@docs length, at

### modify

@docs replaceAt

@docs lowerMinLength

@docs restoreMaxLength

@docs serialize

aliases.name: Arr
aliases.comment:
An `Arr` describes an array where you know more about the amount of elements.


## types

  - amount >= 5

```
Arr (Min Nat5) ...
```

  - 2 <= amount <= 12

```
Arr (In Nat2 Nat12) ...
```

  - = 4

```
Arr (Only Nat4) ...
```


### as argument types

  - amount >= 4

```
Arr (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4) max) ...
```

  - 4 <= amount <= 15

```
Arr (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4) Nat15) ...
```

  - = 15

```
Arr (Only Nat15) ...
```

  - any amount

```
Arr range ...
```
aliases.type: Typed.Typed Typed.Checked Internal.Arr.ArrTag Typed.Internal (Internal.Arr.Content length element)

values.name: at
values.comment:
Element at a valid position.

    Arr.from4 1 2 3 4
        |> Arr.at nat1 FirstToLast
    --> 2

    Arr.from4 1 2 3 4
        |> Arr.at nat1 LastToFirst
    --> 3
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin minMinus1 indexMaybeN) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minMinus1) max) element -> element

values.name: drop
values.comment:
After a certain number of elements from one side.

    with6To10Elements
        |> Arr.drop nat2 LastToFirst
    --> : Arr (In Nat4 (Nat10Plus a)) ...

Use `take` `LastToFirst` if you know the exact amount of elements.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN dropped (Nat.Is minTaken Nat.To min) (Nat.Is maxTaken Nat.To max)) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minTaken maxTaken) element

values.name: empty
values.comment:
No elements.

    Arr.empty
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 atLeast0) element
        |> NArr.push ":)"
    --> : Arr (In Nat1 (Nat1Plus atLeast0)) String
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 atLeast0) element

values.name: fold
values.comment:
Reduce an `Arr` in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from5 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.fold FirstToLast (++) ""
    --> "live"

    Arr.from5 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.fold LastToFirst (++) ""
    --> "evil"
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> result -> result) -> result -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> result

values.name: foldWith
values.comment:
A fold where the initial result is the first value.

    Arr.foldWith FirstToLast maximum
        (Arr.from3 234 345 543)
    --> 543
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> element -> element) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minMinus1) max) element -> element

values.name: from1
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 1 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat1 (TypeNats.Nat1Plus a)) element

values.name: from10
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 10 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat10 (TypeNats.Nat10Plus a)) element

values.name: from11
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 11 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat11 (TypeNats.Nat11Plus a)) element

values.name: from12
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 12 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat12 (TypeNats.Nat12Plus a)) element

values.name: from13
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 13 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat13 (TypeNats.Nat13Plus a)) element

values.name: from14
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 14 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat14 (TypeNats.Nat14Plus a)) element

values.name: from15
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 15 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat15 (TypeNats.Nat15Plus a)) element

values.name: from16
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 16 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat16 (TypeNats.Nat16Plus a)) element

values.name: from2
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 2 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat2 (TypeNats.Nat2Plus a)) element

values.name: from3
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 3 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat3 (TypeNats.Nat3Plus a)) element

values.name: from4
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 4 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat4 (TypeNats.Nat4Plus a)) element

values.name: from5
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 5 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat5 (TypeNats.Nat5Plus a)) element

values.name: from6
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 6 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat6 (TypeNats.Nat6Plus a)) element

values.name: from7
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 7 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat7 (TypeNats.Nat7Plus a)) element

values.name: from8
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 8 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat8 (TypeNats.Nat8Plus a)) element

values.name: from9
values.comment: Create an `Arr` from exactly 9 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat9 (TypeNats.Nat9Plus a)) element

values.name: fromArray
values.comment:
Create an `Arr` from an `Array`. As every `Array` has `>= 0` elements:

    Arr.fromArray arrayFromSomewhere
    --> : Arr (Min Nat0)

    Arr.fromArray
        (Array.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ])
    --> big no

    Arr.from7 0 1 2 3 4 5 6
    --> ok

    Arr.nats nat7
    --> big yes

Tell the compiler if you know the amount of elements. Make sure the it knows as much as you!
values.type: Array.Array element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min TypeNats.Nat0) element

values.name: groupsOf
values.comment:
Split the `Arr` into equal-sized chunks.

    { groups : the Arr divided into equal-sized Arrs
    , less : values to one side which aren't enough
    }

    Arr.from7 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> MinArr.groupsOf nat5 FirstToLast
    --> { groups = Arr.from1 (Arr.from5 1 2 3 4 5)
    --> , less = Arr.from2 6 7
    --> }

The type of the result isn't as accurate as in the example, though!
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minGroupSizMinus1) maxGroupSize groupSizeMaybeN) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> { groups : Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 max) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minGroupSizMinus1) maxGroupSize) element), less : Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 maxGroupSize) element }

values.name: length
values.comment: The amount of elements.
values.type: Arr.Arr length element -> Nat.Nat length

values.name: lowerMinLength
values.comment:
Use a lower minimum length in the type.

    [ atLeast3Elements
    , atLeast4Elements
    ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are
> `Arr (Min Nat3) ...`

    [ atLeast3Elements
    , atLeast4Elements
        |> Arr.lowerMinLength nat3
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn lowerMin min lowerMaybeN) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowerMin max) element

values.name: map
values.comment:
Change every element.

    aToZ =
        Arr.nats nat26
            |> Arr.map
                (val >> fromPositionInABC)

    fromPositionInABC =
        (+) ('a' |> Char.toCode)
            >> Char.fromCode

`val` refers to `Typed.val`.
values.type: (aElement -> bElement) -> Arr.Arr length aElement -> Arr.Arr length bElement

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Combine the elements of 2 `Arr`s to a new element.
If one list is longer, the extra elements are dropped.

    teamLifes aBoard bBoard =
        Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lifes + b.lifes)
            aBoard
            bBoard

The length pf all `Arr`s must be in the same range.

  - If 1 `Arr` is a potential `Arr (Min ...)`, you have to

```
Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lifes + b.lifes)
    (aBoard |> MinArr.value)
    (bBoard |> MinArr.value)

aBoard, bBoard : Arr (In Nat2 someMax) Field
```

for every non-`Arr (Min ...)`.

  - If 1 `Arr` has a higher minimum length, you have to

```
Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lifes + b.lifes)
    (aBoard |> Arr.lowerMinLength nat2)
    bBoard

aBoard : Arr (In Nat5 Nat10) Field
```
values.type: (a -> b -> combined) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length combined

values.name: map3
values.comment: Works like [map2](Arr#map2).
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> combinedElement) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length c -> Arr.Arr length combinedElement

values.name: map4
values.comment: Works like [map2](Arr#map2).
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> combinedElement) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length c -> Arr.Arr length d -> Arr.Arr length combinedElement

values.name: nats
values.comment:
Increasing natural numbers. In the end, there are `length` numbers.

    Arr.nats nat10
    --> : Arr
    -->     (N Nat10 ...)
    -->     (Nat (In Nat0 (Nat9Plus a)))

    from first length_ =
        Arr.nats length_
            |> Arr.map (InNat.add first)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus maxLengthMinus1) lengthMaybeN) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus maxLengthMinus1)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 maxLengthMinus1))

values.name: random
values.comment: Generate a given `amount` of elements and put them in an `Arr`.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max maybeN) -> Random.Generator element -> Random.Generator (Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element)

values.name: repeat
values.comment:
An `Arr` with a given amount of same elements.

    Arr.repeat nat4 'L'
    --> Arr.from4 'L' 'L' 'L' 'L'
    --> : Arr (In Nat4 (Nat4Plus a)) Char


    Arr.repeat atLeast3 'L'
    --> : Arr (Min Nat3) Char
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max maybeN) -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element

values.name: replaceAt
values.comment:
Set the element at an index in a direction.

    Arr.from3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> Arr.replaceAt nat2 FirstToLast "confusion"
    --> Arr.from3 "I" "am" "confusion"

    Arr.from3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> Arr.replaceAt nat1 LastToFirst "feel"
    --> Arr.from3 "I" "feel" "ok"
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin minLengthMinus1 indexMaybeN) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) max) element

values.name: resize
values.comment:
Resize an `Arr` in a direction, padding with a given value.

    Arr.resize LastToFirst nat4 0
        (Arr.from2 1 2)
    --> Array.from4 0 0 1 2

    Arr.resize LastToFirst nat2 0
        (Array.from3 1 2 3)
    --> Array.from2 2 3

    Arr.resize FirstToLast nat4 0
        (Array.from2 1 2)
    --> Array.from4 1 2 0 0

    Arr.resize FirstToLast nat2 0
        (Array.from3 1 2 3)
    --> Array.from2 1 2

This is a quick way to gain some type-level knowledge about the length.
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max maybeN) -> element -> Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element

values.name: restoreMaxLength
values.comment:
Make an `Arr` with a fixed maximum length fit into functions with require a higher maximum length.

While designing argument annotations as general as possible:

    atMost18Elements : Arr (In min Nat18) ...

The argument in `atMost18Elements` should also fit in `atMost19Elements`.

    atMost19Elements theArgument
    --> error :(

    atMost19Elements
        (theArgument |> Arr.restoreMaxLength nat18)
    --> works
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN max (Nat.Is a Nat.To atLeastMax) x) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min atLeastMax) element

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Flip the order of the elements.

    Arr.from5 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.reverse
    --> Arr.from5 "e" "v" "i" "l"
values.type: Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr length element

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s with a specific amount of elements.

    import Serialize


    -- we can't start if we have no worlds to choose from!
    serializeGameRow :
        Serialize.Codec
            String
            (Arr (Only Nat10) GameField)
    serializeGameRow =
        Arr.serialize nat10 serializeGameField

    encode : Arr (Only Nat10) GameField -> Bytes
    encode =
        Serialize.encodeToBytes serializeGameRow

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (Only Nat10) GameField)
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serializeGameRow
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max maybeN) -> Serialize.Codec String.String element -> Serialize.Codec String.String (Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element)

values.name: take
values.comment:
This works for both

    Arr.from8 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> Arr.take nat3 nat3 LastToFirst
    --> Arr.from3 5 6 7

    Arr.from8 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> Arr.take between3To7 nat7 FirstToLast
    --> : Arr (In Nat3 Nat7) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minTaken maxTaken takenMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN maxTaken (Nat.Is a Nat.To atLeastMaxTaken) (Nat.Is maxTakenToMin Nat.To min)) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minTaken atLeastMaxTaken) element

values.name: toArray
values.comment:
Convert the `Arr` to an `Array`.
Just do this in the end.
Try to keep extra information as long as you can.

    Arr.nats nat5
        |> Arr.map val
        |> Arr.toArray
    --> Array.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: Arr.Arr length element -> Array.Array element

name: InArr
comment:
If the maximum length is set to a specific value (also in `ArgOnly`),

    -- only up to 50 tags
    tag :
        Arr (In min Nat50 maybeN) String
        -> a
        -> Tagged a

use these operations instead of the ones in `Arr` or `MinArr`.


## modify

@docs push, extend, extendOnly, removeAt, insertAt


## scan length

@docs isLengthInRange, isLength, isLengthAtLeast, isLengthAtMost


## extra

@docs serialize

values.name: extend
values.comment: Append the elements of another `Arr (In ...)`.
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In extensionMin extensionMax) element -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN extensionMin (Nat.Is min Nat.To extendedMin) x) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN extensionMax (Nat.Is max Nat.To extendedMax) y) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In extendedMin extendedMax) element

values.name: extendOnly
values.comment: Append the elements of an `Arr (Only ...)`.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN added (Nat.Is min Nat.To sumMin) (Nat.Is max Nat.To sumMax)) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Only added) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In sumMin sumMax) element

values.name: insertAt
values.comment: Use `MinArr.push`, if the `max` isn't known to be a certain value.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin minMinus1 indexMaybeN) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minMinus1) max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat2Plus minMinus1) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus max)) element

values.name: isLength
values.comment:
Compare the length to an exact `Nat (ArgN ...)` length.
Are there `more`, `less` or an `equal` amount of elements?

`min` ensures that the `Nat (ArgN ...)` is greater than the minimum length.

    chooseFormation :
        Arr (In Nat0 Nat50) Character
        -> Formation
    chooseFormation characters =
        characters
            |> InArr.isLength nat7
                { min = nat0 }
                { equal = SpecialAttack
                , less = Retreat
                , more = Fight
                }

    type Formation
        = SpecialAttack (Arr (Only Nat7) Character)
        | Retreat (Arr (In Nat0 Nat6) Character)
        | Fight (Arr (In Nat8 Nat50) Character)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN tried (Nat.Is triedToMax Nat.To max) (Nat.Is a Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus atLeastTriedMinus1))) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN min (Nat.Is minToTried Nat.To tried) x) } -> { equal : Arr.Arr (Nat.Only tried) element -> result, greater : Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat2Plus triedMinus1) max) element -> result, less : Arr.Arr (Nat.In min atLeastTriedMinus1) element -> result } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> result

values.name: isLengthAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the length

  - `equalOrGreater` than a lower bound or

  - `less`?

`min` ensures that the lower bound is greater than the minimum length.

    first5 :
        Arr (In min max maybeN) element
        -> Maybe (Arr (In Nat5 max maybeN) element)
    first5 =
        MinArr.lowerMinLength nat0
            >> MinArr.isLengthAtLeast nat5
                { min = nat0 }
                { less = \_ -> Nothing
                , equalOrGreater = Just
                }
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN lowerBound (Nat.Is a Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus atLeastTriedMinus1)) (Nat.Is atLeastRange Nat.To max)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN min (Nat.Is (TypeNats.Nat1Plus lessRange) Nat.To lowerBound) x) } -> { less : Arr.Arr (Nat.In min atLeastTriedMinus1) element -> result, equalOrGreater : Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowerBound max) element -> result } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> result

values.name: isLengthAtMost
values.comment:
Is the length

  - `equalOrLess` than a upper bound or

  - `greater`?

`min` ensures that the upper bound is greater than the minimum length.

    -- at least 3 and only up to 50 tags
    tag :
        Arr (In (Nat3Plus orHigherMin) Nat50 maybeN) String
        -> a
        -> Tagged a

    tagIfValidTags :
        Arr (In (Nat3Plus orHigherMin) max maybeN)
        -> a
        -> Maybe (Tagged a)
    tagIfValidTags array value =
        array
            |> Arr.fromArray
            |> InArr.isLengthAtMost nat50
                { equalOrLess = tag value >> Just
                , greater = \_ -> Nothing
                }
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN upperBound (Nat.Is a Nat.To atLeastUpperBound) (Nat.Is (TypeNats.Nat1Plus greaterRange) Nat.To max)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN min (Nat.Is minToUpperBound Nat.To upperBound) x) } -> { equalOrLess : Arr.Arr (Nat.In min atLeastUpperBound) element -> result, greater : Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus upperBound) max) element -> result } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> result

values.name: isLengthInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range from a lower to an upper bound, is the length

  - `inRange`

  - `greater` than the upper bound or

  - `less` than the lower bound?

`min` ensures that the lower bound is greater than the minimum length.

    chooseFormation :
        Arr (In Nat0 Nat50) Character
        -> Formation
    chooseFormation characters =
        characters
            |> InArr.isLengthInRange nat10
                nat20
                { min = nat0 }
                { equal = SpecialAttack
                , less = Retreat
                , more = Fight
                }

    type Formation
        = SpecialAttack (Arr (In Nat10 Nat20) Character)
        | Retreat (Arr (In Nat0 Nat9) Character)
        | Fight (Arr (In Nat21 Nat50) Character)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN lowerBound (Nat.Is lowerBoundToUpperBound Nat.To upperBound) (Nat.Is lowerBoundA Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus atLeastLowerBoundMinus1))) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN upperBound (Nat.Is upperBoundToMax Nat.To max) (Nat.Is upperBoundA Nat.To atLeastUpperBound)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN min (Nat.Is minToLowerBound Nat.To lowerBound) x) } -> { inRange : Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowerBound atLeastUpperBound) element -> result, less : Arr.Arr (Nat.In min atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) element -> result, greater : Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus upperBound) max) element -> result } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> result

values.name: push
values.comment:
Put a new element after the others.

    arrWith5To10Elements
        |> InArr.push "becomes the last"
    --> : Arr (In Nat6 Nat11) String
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus min) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus max)) element

values.name: removeAt
values.comment: Kick an element out of an Arr at a given index in a direction.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin minMinus1 indexMaybeN) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minMinus1) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus maxMinus1)) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minMinus1 maxMinus1) element

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s within a minimum & maximum length.

    import Serialize

    serialize10To15Ints :
        Serialize.Codec
            String
            (Arr (In Nat10 (Nat15Plus a)) Int)
    serialize10To15Ints =
        InArr.serialize Serialize.int nat10 nat15

    encode :
        Arr (In (Nat10Plus orHigherMin) Nat15 maybeN) Int
        -> Bytes
    encode =
        InNat.value
            >> Serialize.encodeToBytes serialize10To15Ints

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (In Nat10 (Nat15Plus a) Int))
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serialize10To15Ints

For decoded `Arr`s with a length outside of the expected bounds, the `Result` is an error message.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLowerBound upperBound lowerBoundMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn upperBound upperBoundPlusA upperBoundMaybeN) -> Serialize.Codec String.String element -> Serialize.Codec String.String (Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLowerBound upperBoundPlusA) element)

name: MinArr

values.name: extend
values.comment:
Append an `Arr (In ...)`.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> MinArr.extend nat3 arrWithAtLeast3Elements
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) ...
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In extensionMin extensionMax) element -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN extensionMin (Nat.Is min Nat.To extendedMin) x) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min extendedMin) element

values.name: insertAt
values.comment:
Put a new element at an index in a direction.

    arrWithAtLeast5Elements
        |> MinArr.insertAt nat0 FirstToLast
            "becomes the first"
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) String
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin minLengthMinus1 indexMaybeN) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (TypeNats.Nat2Plus minLengthMinus1)) element

values.name: isLength
values.comment:
Compare the length to an exact `Nat (ArgN ...)`.
Is it `greater`, `less` or `equal`?

`min` ensures that the `Nat (ArgN ...)` is bigger than the minimum length.

    convertUserArguments :
        String
        -> Result String (Arr (Only Nat3))
    convertUserArguments =
        String.words
            >> Array.fromList
            >> Arr.fromArray
            >> MinArr.isLength nat3
                { min = nat0 }
                { equal = Ok
                , less =
                    \less ->
                        Err
                            ("I need 3 arguments, but there are only "
                                ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length less))
                                ++ "."
                            )
                , greater =
                    \more ->
                        Err
                            ("I need exact 3 arguments, so "
                                ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length more))
                                ++ " is too much."
                            )
                }
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN (TypeNats.Nat1Plus triedMinus1) (Nat.Is a Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus triedMinus1PlusA)) x) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN min (Nat.Is minToTriedMinus1 Nat.To triedMinus1) y) } -> { equal : Arr.Arr (Nat.Only (TypeNats.Nat1Plus triedMinus1)) element -> result, greater : Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (TypeNats.Nat2Plus triedMinus1)) element -> result, less : Arr.Arr (Nat.In min triedMinus1PlusA) element -> result } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> result

values.name: isLengthAtLeast
values.comment:
Compare the length to an exact `Nat (ArgN ...)`.
Is it `equalOrGreater` or `less`?

`min` ensures that the lower bound is bigger than the minimum length.

    convertUserArguments :
        String
        -> Result String (Arr (Min Nat3))
    convertUserArguments =
        String.words
            >> Array.fromList
            >> Arr.fromArray
            >> MinArr.isLengthAtLeast nat3
                { min = nat0 }
                { equalOrGreater = Ok
                , less =
                    \less ->
                        Err
                            ("I need at least 3 arguments, but there are only "
                                ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length less))
                                ++ "."
                            )
                }
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN lowerBound (Nat.Is a Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus lowerBoundMinus1PlusA)) x) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN min (Nat.Is minToTriedMin Nat.To lowerBound) y) } -> { equalOrGreater : Arr.Arr (Nat.Min lowerBound) element -> result, less : Arr.Arr (Nat.In min lowerBoundMinus1PlusA) element -> result } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> result

values.name: isLengthAtMost
values.comment:
Is the length

  - `equalOrLess` than an upper bound or

  - `greater`?

`min` ensures that the upper bound is greater than the minimum length.

    -- only up to 50 tags
    tag :
        Arr (In min Nat50 maybeN) String
        -> a
        -> Tagged a

    tagIfValidTags : Array String -> a -> Maybe (Tagged a)
    tagIfValidTags array value =
        array
            |> Arr.fromArray
            |> MinArr.isLengthAtMost nat50
                { equalOrLess = tag value >> Just
                , greater = \_ -> Nothing
                }
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN upperBound (Nat.Is a Nat.To upperBoundPlusA) x) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN min (Nat.Is minToAtMostMin Nat.To upperBound) y) } -> { equalOrLess : Arr.Arr (Nat.In min upperBoundPlusA) element -> result, greater : Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (TypeNats.Nat1Plus upperBound)) element -> result } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> result

values.name: push
values.comment:
Put a new element after the others.

    arrWithAtLeast5Elements
        |> MinArr.push "becomes the last"
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) String
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (TypeNats.Nat1Plus min)) element

values.name: removeAt
values.comment:
Kick out the element at an index in a direction.

    removeLast arrWithAtLeast1Element =
        arrWithAtLeast1Element
            |> MinArr.removeAt nat0 LastToFirst
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin minLengthMinus1 indexMaybeExact) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLengthMinus1 maxLength) element

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s with a minimum amount of elements.

    import Serialize


    -- we can't start if we have no worlds to choose from!
    serializeSaves :
        Serialize.Codec
            String
            (Arr (Min Nat1) World)
    serializeSaves =
        MinArr.serialize nat1 serializeWorld

    encode :
        Arr (In (Nat1Plus minMinus1) max maybeN) World
        -> Bytes
    encode =
        MinArr.value
            >> Serialize.encodeToBytes serializeSaves

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (Min Nat1) World)
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serializeSaves
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgN lowerBound (Nat.Is a Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus lowerBoundMinus1PlusA)) x) -> Serialize.Codec String.String element -> Serialize.Codec String.String (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min lowerBound) element)

values.name: value
values.comment:
Convert an exact Arr (In min ...) to a Nat (Min min).

    between4And10Elements |> MinArr.value
    --> : Arr (Min Nat4) ...

There is only 1 situation you should use this.

To make these the same type.

    [ atLeast1Element, between1And10Elements ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are
> `Arr (Min Nat1) ...`

    [ atLeast1Element
    , between1And10Elements |> MinArr.value
    ]
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min min) element
